title: berteroa obliqua subsp. obliqua
intro: Berteroa obliqua subsp. obliqua, also known as the Hoary Alison or Hoary Cress, is a flowering plant species in the mustard family native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Berteroa obliqua subsp. obliqua include relief from respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, but more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
